refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety constraints) to enable proper allocation and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, standard earthmoving task). Measure actual output (m³/h or ton/h) against target. Cross-check against safety incident log and operational policy compliance. Establish baseline and variance acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints (geofence, load limits, stability) to prevent safety policy violations during productive operations.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (cycle count, material moved, time on task) for KPI verification and continuous improvement.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Optimize vehicle hydraulic system and bucket geometry to minimize cycle time; pair with local operator interface for direct control.
DP-B: Implement high-fidelity teleoperation with low-latency video/sensor feedback and predictive control algorithms; enable remote operation to reduce operator fatigue and improve consistency.
DP-C: Integrate real-time productivity monitoring (GNSS, load cell, cycle timer) with automated safety enforcement (geofence, stability limits) to maintain KPI while respecting safety constraints.
DP-D: Combine vehicle performance optimization (DP-A), teleoperation capability (DP-B), and safety/productivity monitoring (DP-C) into a unified NGHE solution architecture.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Integrated NGHE solution combining optimized vehicle performance, teleoperation-ready architecture, and real-time safety-constrained productivity monitoring. This DP addresses the full scope of CR-NGHE-001 by enabling both on-site and remote operation modes, enforcing safety policies, and providing measurable productivity feedback.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time optimization (DP-A) is independent of operator interface choice (local vs. remote, DP-B).
Safety/productivity monitoring (DP-C) is decoupled from vehicle mechanics; it observes and constrains, not redesigns.
Teleoperation (DP-B) adds latency and control complexity but does not require vehicle redesign if architecture is prepared.
Minor coupling: teleoperation fidelity (video, sensor bandwidth) depends on Network Link interface capacity; this is a known constraint, not a design coupling.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is a composition of three loosely coupled sub-DPs. Each can be developed and verified independently, then integrated. No circular dependencies detected.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle hydraulic system and bucket geometry can be optimized to meet target_cycle_time without major structural redesign.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency (network + control loop) can be kept ≤ operator reaction time threshold (~200–500 ms) for safe remote operation.
Assumption: Safety sensors (GNSS, load cell, inclinometer) are available and reliable in the target worksite environment.
Risk: If target_cycle_time is aggressive, vehicle optimization alone may be insufficient; operator skill/consistency becomes critical.
Risk: Teleoperation over unreliable network links may degrade productivity or create safety hazards; fallback to local operation required.
Risk: Safety constraint enforcement (geofence, stability limits) may conflict with productivity targets if thresholds are overly conservative.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-D to explicitly state fallback modes: (1) Local operation as primary mode with teleoperation as optional enhancement; (2) Safety constraints as hard limits, not soft suggestions; (3) Productivity measurement as post-hoc KPI, not real-time control target. This clarifies that safety is non-negotiable and productivity is an outcome, not a constraint.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their orchestration. It owns the end-to-end productivity KPI delivery, safety policy enforcement, and operator experience (local or remote). It is the system-level module responsible for allocating sub-requirements to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, and for integrating their outputs via Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity telemetry (cycle count, material volume, time on task, vehicle state) to external systems (Site Manager dashboard, analytics platform). Frequency: ~1 Hz or event-driven.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task assignments (dig zone, load target, dump location) from Site Manager or autonomous planner. Tasking is translated into vehicle control commands and operator guidance.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits safety alerts (geofence breach, stability warning, load limit exceeded) to Safety Manager and operator. Alerts trigger corrective actions or emergency stop.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link for teleoperation command/feedback (if remote mode active) and for cloud-based telemetry/tasking. Bandwidth and latency are critical for teleoperation fidelity.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for CR-NGHE-001. It owns the productivity KPI, integrates all subsystems, and orchestrates safety/teleoperation. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. All internal logic (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety monitoring) is contained.

Vehicle owns the mechanical/hydraulic subsystem and cycle time optimization (DP-A). However, it does not own teleoperation logic, safety monitoring, or productivity measurement. If assigned here, these concerns would diffuse to Teleoperation and Safety Services, creating unclear ownership. Vehicle is a sub-module of NGHE Solution, not a peer.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and control latency (DP-B). However, it does not own vehicle performance, safety policy enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 here would fragment the KPI responsibility. Teleoperation is a sub-module of NGHE Solution.

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement (geofence, stability limits, DP-C). However, it does not own vehicle performance or teleoperation. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 here would make Safety Services responsible for productivity, which is a category error. Safety Services is a sub-module of NGHE Solution.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 (Productivity KPI) is a system-level requirement that spans vehicle performance, operator interface, safety constraints, and measurement. No single sub-module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) can own it independently. NGHE Solution, as the top-level integration module, is the correct owner. It allocates sub-requirements to Vehicle (cycle time), Teleoperation (operator interface), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement), then integrates their outputs to deliver the KPI.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is too abstract, ownership of integration logic (orchestration, fallback modes, mode switching) may be unclear. Mitigation: Define explicit integration sub-modules or state machines within NGHE Solution.

Risk: Teleoperation and Safety Services may have conflicting constraints (e.g., low-latency teleoperation vs. safety-critical monitoring). Mitigation: Establish priority rules and interface contracts at NGHE Solution level.

Risk: Productivity measurement may require Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to emit compatible telemetry. Mitigation: Define Telemetry interface schema at NGHE Solution level; enforce compliance in sub-modules.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify that NGHE Solution owns the integration logic and KPI delivery, but delegates sub-concerns to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Define explicit sub-requirements for each sub-module (FR-NGHE-001.1 → Vehicle, FR-NGHE-001.2 → Teleoperation, FR-NGHE-001.3 → Safety Services, FR-NGHE-001.4 → NGHE Solution integration). This makes ownership and interface contracts explicit.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI with safety compliance) is directly addressed by DP-D (integrated solution combining vehicle optimization, teleoperation, and safety monitoring). The mapping is clear: vehicle cycle time → productivity; operator interface → task execution; safety constraints → policy compliance; telemetry → KPI measurement. No functional gaps or mismatches detected.

diffusion_risks: DP-D is a composition of three sub-DPs (vehicle optimization, teleoperation, safety monitoring). If NGHE Solution IM is too coarse, integration logic may diffuse to sub-modules. Mitigation: Define explicit integration sub-requirements and interface contracts.

Teleoperation and Safety Services may have overlapping concerns (e.g., both need real-time vehicle state). Mitigation: Establish clear data ownership and publish-subscribe patterns via Telemetry interface.
Network Link is a shared resource for both teleoperation and telemetry. Bandwidth contention may degrade both. Mitigation: Prioritize safety alerts over productivity telemetry; define QoS policies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM contains all logic for DP-D. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and do not leak concerns outside. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal. Containment is sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure output (m³/h or ton/h), check safety compliance, confirm telemetry accuracy. Acceptance criteria are quantitative (≥ productivity_target) and observable. No hidden or unmeasurable requirements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Refined FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM with clear sub-allocations to Vehicle (FR-NGHE-001.1), Teleoperation (FR-NGHE-001.2), Safety Services (FR-NGHE-001.3), and NGHE Solution integration (FR-NGHE-001.4). Each sub-FR is traceable to a sub-DP and a responsible sub-module. Allocation is clear and complete.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit acceptance criteria for each sub-FR (e.g., target_cycle_time for FR-NGHE-001.1, max_latency for FR-NGHE-001.2, geofence_accuracy for FR-NGHE-001.3).
Establish interface contracts for Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link (schema, frequency, latency, reliability).
Define fallback modes and mode-switching logic at NGHE Solution level (e.g., if Network Link fails, switch from remote to local operation).
Specify safety constraint thresholds (geofence margin, stability limit, load limit) and their interaction with productivity targets.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that is not atomic. It has been successfully refined into four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4), each addressing a distinct concern (vehicle performance, operator interface, safety constraints, measurement). The refined FR, selected DP, and assigned IM are consistent and pass all gates. The next step is to decompose each sub-FR to the next level, allocating them to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and integration logic respectively. This will enable detailed design and verification.
decision.justification_detail: ZIGZAG_DOWN is appropriate because: (1) Refined FR is clear and verifiable; (2) DP-D is sound and addresses all sub-concerns; (3) NGHE Solution IM is the correct owner with clear sub-allocations; (4) Sub-FRs are ready for next-level decomposition; (5) No blocking risks or design flaws detected at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving operations (dig, load, transport, dump) with cycle time ≤ target_cycle_time to achieve productivity_target. [Allocated to Vehicle IM]

FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (local or remote) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to enable safe, efficient task execution. [Allocated to Teleoperation IM]

FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and enforce safety constraints (geofence, load limits, stability) to prevent safety policy violations during productive operations. [Allocated to Safety Services IM]

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (cycle count, material moved, time on task) for KPI verification and continuous improvement. [Allocated to NGHE Solution integration logic]

assumptions: Vehicle hydraulic system and bucket geometry can be optimized to meet target_cycle_time without major structural redesign.
Teleoperation latency (network + control loop) can be kept ≤ operator reaction time threshold (~200–500 ms) for safe remote operation.
Safety sensors (GNSS, load cell, inclinometer) are available and reliable in the target worksite environment.
Productivity measurement is post-hoc KPI, not real-time control target; safety constraints are hard limits.
Network Link has sufficient bandwidth and reliability for both teleoperation and telemetry (with prioritization rules).
Site Manager and Safety Manager systems can receive and act on Tasking, Telemetry, and SafetyAlert interfaces.
open_risks: If target_cycle_time is aggressive, vehicle optimization alone may be insufficient; operator skill/consistency becomes critical. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study on vehicle performance limits.
Teleoperation over unreliable network links may degrade productivity or create safety hazards; fallback to local operation required. Mitigation: Define network QoS requirements and fallback logic.

Safety constraint enforcement (geofence, stability limits) may conflict with productivity targets if thresholds are overly conservative. Mitigation: Establish safety-productivity trade-off rules with stakeholders.
Teleoperation and Safety Services may have overlapping concerns (e.g., both need real-time vehicle state). Mitigation: Establish clear data ownership and publish-subscribe patterns.
Network Link is a shared resource for both teleoperation and telemetry; bandwidth contention may degrade both. Mitigation: Prioritize safety alerts over productivity telemetry; define QoS policies.
evidence_needed: Feasibility study: Vehicle cycle time optimization potential (hydraulic system, bucket geometry, operator technique).
Network characterization: Latency, bandwidth, reliability in target worksite environment (cellular, satellite, local mesh).
Safety sensor validation: GNSS accuracy, load cell calibration, inclinometer reliability in earthmoving operations.
Operator feedback: Situational awareness requirements for local vs. remote operation; acceptable latency thresholds.
Safety policy documentation: Geofence margins, load limits, stability thresholds, and their interaction with productivity targets.
Telemetry schema: Cycle count, material volume, time on task, vehicle state; frequency and accuracy requirements for KPI measurement.
